messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure and reference files.
Let me check existing overrides and shared UI imports for patterns:
Files already exist. Let me read the current implementations and check tests:
Good, the implementation files already exist. Now let me check the test files:
Implementation and tests already exist. Let me run the tests to verify they pass:
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server starts correctly and resolves all modules:
Dev server returns HTTP 200 — all modules resolve correctly. Let me now check the validation hook and ensure it's set up:

The validation hook exists and doesn't need changes for this feature since no new routes were created — the Quick View is a modal overlay on existing PLP pages.  Now let me do the SSR safety checklist:
Intent: The system instructions mandate wrapping `ProductView` in an ErrorBoundary when rendered inside a custom modal container. Let me check if `react-error-boundary` is available and add it:
I'll create a simple ErrorBoundary class component directly and wrap ProductView. Let me update the QuickViewModal:
Now re-run the tests to make sure everything still passes:

All 26 tests pass. The uncovered lines 34,38 are the ErrorBoundary's `getDerivedStateFromError` and error render path, which is expected (those only trigger on actual render crashes).  Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The `storefront-dev` phase for `product-quick-view` is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Summary  ### Files Delivered (4 files)  | File | Action | Description | |---|---|---| | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | CREATE | ProductTile override with Quick View overlay bar | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | CREATE | 12 unit tests for tile overlay bar | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | CREATE + ENHANCED | QuickViewModal with ErrorBoundary wrapping ProductView | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | CREATE | 14 unit tests for modal shell, content, accessibility |  ### Key Implementation Details  1. **ProductTile Override**: Wraps the base `ProductTile` in a `role="group"` container with an absolutely-positioned Quick View overlay bar that slides up on hover (desktop) and is always visible (mobile). Uses `ViewIcon` + "Quick View" text on a semi-transparent dark background.  2. **QuickViewModal**: Uses `useProductViewModal` hook to fetch full product data, renders a Chakra `Modal` with three states:    - **Loading**: Centered `Spinner` with `data-testid="quick-view-spinner"`    - **Error/Unavailable**: Warning message with `data-testid="quick-view-error"`    - **Success**: `ProductView` with `showFullLink={true}`, `imageSize="sm"`, wrapped in `QuickViewErrorBoundary`  3. **ErrorBoundary**: Class component wrapping `ProductView` inside the modal portal to prevent render crashes from destroying the entire page (mandatory per system instructions).  4. **Corner cases handled**: Product sets/bundles excluded, missing `productId` guard, `e.preventDefault()` + `e.stopPropagation()` on bar click, accessible aria-labels, keyboard navigation, SSR-safe.  ### Verification Results - ✅ **26/26 unit tests passing** - ✅ **Dev server HTTP 200** (all webpack modules resolve) - ✅ **SSR safety**: No `window`/`document` access, no `Date.now()`/`Math.random()`

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me look at the actual source files that were created for this feature:
Good - I can see the source components and existing test files. Let me run the tests to see their current status:
All 26 tests pass. Let me check the uncovered lines to improve coverage:

Tests pass, but I see coverage gaps at the ErrorBoundary (lines 34, 38 in quick-view-modal) and branch coverage on product name fallback (line 28 in product-tile). Let me also check the spec for additional tests the previous author may have missed. I need to add:  1. **QuickViewModal**: ErrorBoundary test (catches render errors), `imageSize="sm"` prop test 2. **ProductTile**: Test with `name` fallback (no `productName`), style assertion tests
Now update the ProductTile tests to add coverage for `name` fallback and style assertions:
Now let's run all the tests to verify everything passes:
All 34 tests pass with **100% coverage** across statements, branches, functions, and lines for both components. Let me also quickly verify that the full test suite still passes:
One test suite failed but it's not mine. Let me check what failed:

The only failure is an E2E Playwright test (`e2e/storefront-smoke.spec.ts`) that can't even parse because Playwright isn't installed in the Jest runtime — this is expected since Playwright tests run separately via a different runner. My unit tests are completely clean.  Now let me commit and mark the pipeline phase complete:

The storefront-unit-test phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:  ## Summary  ### Tests Written: 34 total (17 per component), 100% coverage  **QuickViewModal (`quick-view-modal/index.test.js`) — 17 tests:** - Modal shell: spinner during fetch, ProductView when loaded, correct `data-testid`, closed state - Accessibility: `aria-label` with product name, fallback to generic text, prefers fetched name over search hit - Content: product data passed to ProductView, "View Full Details" link, Add to Cart button, `showFullLink={true}`, `imageSize="sm"` prop - Interactions: close button calls `onClose`, Escape key closes modal - Error handling: unavailable product shows error state, ErrorBoundary catches ProductView render crashes - Loading: `isProductLoading` forwarded correctly  **ProductTile Override (`product-tile/index.test.js`) — 17 tests:** - Overlay bar rendering: visible on standard products, contains ViewIcon + "Quick View" text, correct `aria-label` - Exclusions: no bar for product sets, bundles, or missing `productId`; base tile still renders for excluded types - Interactions: clicking opens modal, `preventDefault` + `stopPropagation` called, modal hides on close - Name fallbacks: uses `name` when `productName` missing, empty string when both missing - Accessibility: `role="group"` on container, semantic `<button>` element - Prop forwarding: all props passed to base ProductTile

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now I have full understanding of the feature. Let me create the E2E test file.  **Key data-testid contract:** - `quick-view-btn` — overlay bar button on product tiles - `quick-view-modal` — modal content wrapper - `quick-view-spinner` — loading spinner inside modal - `quick-view-error` — error/unavailable state in modal
Now let me run the self-review gate and verify the file:
Self-review gate passed — no banned patterns (`networkidle`, `waitForTimeout`) found. The test file has 333 lines with 7 test cases. Now let me commit and complete.

The `e2e-author` phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was created:  ## E2E Test File: `e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts`  **7 test cases** covering the Product Quick View feature:  | # | Test | What it verifies | |---|---|---| | 1 | Quick View button renders on PLP | `data-testid="quick-view-btn"` is visible after hovering a product tile | | 2 | Clicking opens modal with content | Three-outcome assertion: modal content / error state / crash page | | 3 | Modal closes with close button | Chakra `ModalCloseButton` (aria-label="Close") hides the modal | | 4 | Modal closes with Escape key | `page.keyboard.press('Escape')` hides the modal | | 5 | Button has accessible aria-label | `aria-label` matches `/^Quick View\s/` (includes product name) | | 6 | Quick View doesn't navigate away | URL remains unchanged after modal open/close | | 7 | Modal shows spinner → content/error | Verifies loading lifecycle resolves to content or error state |  **Compliance:** - ✅ No `networkidle` or `waitForTimeout` (self-review gate passed) - ✅ `domcontentloaded` for all navigation - ✅ Explicit locator waits with `waitFor({ state: 'visible' })` - ✅ Mandatory browser diagnostics (console errors + failed requests + screenshots) - ✅ PWA Kit crash page detection helper - ✅ Three-outcome assertion pattern for modal interactions - ✅ All selectors use `data-testid` contract: `quick-view-btn`, `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error`
